name: maquette
description: Image-guided brand kits, component libraries, and implemented website pages

interface.shortDescription: Generate brand kits, components, and pages with image-first QA
interface.longDescription: Create website brand kits, reusable component libraries, and implemented pages through an image_gen-first contract-driven workflow.
defaultPrompt: Use $maquette to make a homepage for a fictional sparkling beverage brand called Fizz & Co. Run the full workflow: brand kit, component library, then homepage. Use the Maquette image-worker subagent handoff for generated images when available and allowed, and ask for approval after brand-board and page-concept images. Include product cards, marketing sections, newsletter signup, footer social links, and original generated image assets where useful.

Use $maquette-brand-kit to create a focused foundational website brand kit for a boutique snack brand. Generate and inspect a brand board first, ask whether to use it, make a new one, or revise it, then produce design-system JSON, CSS tokens, and approval notes.

Use $maquette-components to build a reusable component library from the existing approved brand kit. Process focused 1:1 component artifacts sequentially: check optional QA tooling, ask for an install decision when any planned QA capability is blocked including partial installs such as missing ajv-formats, generate one text-only CSS-contract poster by default, transcribe it into a contract CSS bridge file, write concrete category-prefixed componentized batch replica/reference, CSS/JS, catalog, and screenshot-reviewed artifacts, then move to the next artifact. Generate visual component sheets only when explicitly requested or when the CSS-contract route is documented as blocked. Include buttons, forms, cards, product cards, newsletter modules, footer/social modules, responsive navigation, and reference QA.

Use $maquette-pages to create a pricing page from the existing approved brand kit and component library. Reuse existing components first, request focused missing component coverage only if needed, generate and inspect the page concept, ask whether to use it, make a new one, or revise it, write the page layout contract, then implement and QA the page.

Use $maquette-pages to create a product listing page from the existing Maquette artifacts. Check product-card coverage, align repeated card actions, use recognizable footer social icons, record the page layout contract, and record responsive QA.
